tickerText: [TENNIS] Wimbledon 2026 Day 3 at the All England Club Wednesday, July 1: second-round singles begin with defending champion Jannik Sinner facing Nuno Borges and seven-time winner Novak Djokovic meeting Stefanos Tsitsipas on Centre Court, while Barbora Krejcikova tests Roland Garros champion Mirra Andreeva and Aryna Sabalenka, Coco Gauff, and Naomi Osaka headline the grounds after Maya Joint ended Serena Williams' comeback 6-3, 6-7 (6-8), 6-3 on Day 2 • [SQUASH] HCL-SRFI PSA Challenger in Mumbai reaches Round 2 Wednesday at Juhu Vile Parle Gymkhana — India's Shameena Riaz stunned top seed Hayley Ward 8-11, 11-9, 11-9, 11-9 and Om Semwal outlasted third seed Diego Gobbi in five games as 48 players from 15 countries contest the $30,000 combined purse through July 3 • [BADMINTON] YONEX Canada Open 2026 enters main-draw Round of 32 Wednesday at Markham Pan Am Centre — men's singles, women's singles, and mixed doubles open the afternoon session at the $250K BWF Super 300 after qualification wrapped Tuesday, with US Open champion Su Li Yang among the seeded contenders chasing 7,000 ranking points • [PICKLEBALL] Sansan PPA Asia 500 Tokyo Open kicks off July 1–4 at Arena Tachikawa Tachihi — Zane Ford and 18-year-old Kiora Kunimoto lead the singles seedings in a $50,000 stop that follows Beijing and Macao as the Carvana PPA Tour's Asian calendar rolls toward Singapore and the Veolia National Championships in Cary • [TABLE TENNIS] WTT United States Smash 2026 moves into Round of 32 Wednesday at the Ontario Convention Center — defending champion Wang Chuqin faces India's Manav Thakkar after sweeping Zhou Qihao, Kanak Jha carries home-court momentum after upsetting No. 14 Shunsuke Togami, and No. 6 Chen Yi fell to Ying Han as the $1.55M Grand Smash runs through July 5

newsItems.title: Djokovic Meets Tsitsipas as Wimbledon Day 3 Opens at SW19
newsItems.excerpt: The Championships enter Day 3 on Wednesday, July 1, with second-round singles action across the All England Club. Centre Court hosts the marquee men's clash between seven-time Wimbledon champion Novak Djokovic and former ATP Finals winner Stefanos Tsitsipas, bookended by defending men's champion Jannik Sinner against Nuno Borges and a women's blockbuster between 2024 Wimbledon winner Barbora Krejcikova and Roland Garros champion Mirra Andreeva. World No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka, Coco Gauff, Naomi Osaka, Daniil Medvedev, and Félix Auger-Aliassime also return on the outer courts. Day 2 delivered one of the fortnight's biggest stories when 20-year-old Australian Maya Joint defeated Serena Williams 6-3, 6-7 (6-8), 6-3 in the 44-year-old's first singles match since 2022, saving a match point in the second-set tiebreak before prevailing in two hours and 22 minutes on Centre Court.

newsItems.title: Indian Upsets Shake Mumbai PSA Challenger on Day Two
newsItems.excerpt: Round 2 at the HCL-SRFI PSA Challenger Squash Tournament produced two of the biggest shocks so far at the Juhu Vile Parle Gymkhana Club in Mumbai. India's Shameena Riaz toppled top seed Hayley Ward of South Africa 8-11, 11-9, 11-9, 11-9 and will next face Japan's sixth seed Akari Midorikawa. Om Semwal displayed discipline and stamina to outlast third seed Diego Gobbi of Brazil 6-11, 11-6, 11-9, 9-11, 11-9, setting up a quarterfinal against Egypt's Ziad Ibrahim, who beat India's fifth seed Suraj Kumar Chand 11-4, 11-6, 11-5. Third seed Tanvi Khanna advanced with a straight-games win over Pooja Arthi Raghu. The $30,000 combined men's and women's event runs through July 3 with 48 players from 15 countries, led by Hong Kong's Alex Lau and South Africa's Hayley Ward among the pre-tournament favorites.

newsItems.title: Tokyo Open Opens as PPA Tour Asia Lands in Japan's Capital
newsItems.excerpt: The Sansan PPA Asia 500 Tokyo Open begins Wednesday, July 1, at Arena Tachikawa Tachihi in western Tokyo, marking the Carvana PPA Tour's first elite stop in Japan's capital. The four-day event carries a $50,000 prize pool and 500 ranking points per discipline, with Zane Ford seeded first in men's singles after a runner-up finish at the Beijing Open and 18-year-old Kiora Kunimoto leading the women's draw. Macao Open champion Tama Shimabukuro is the men's second seed and tops the men's doubles bracket alongside Yuta Funemizu. Beijing Open winner Sahra Dennehy pairs with MLP star Danni-Elle Townsend as the top women's doubles seed. More than 600 players registered across pro and amateur brackets as Japan's rapidly growing pickleball scene hosts its highest-profile international event yet, with Singapore, Ho Chi Minh City, and Shenzhen next on the 2026 Asian calendar.

newsItems.title: Kanak Jha Stuns Togami as WTT US Smash Reaches Round of 32
newsItems.excerpt: Main-draw action at the WTT United States Smash 2026 continued at the Ontario Convention Center in Southern California as the $1.55 million Grand Smash moved into Round of 32 play. Defending men's champion and world No. 1 Wang Chuqin opened his title defense with an 11-6, 15-13, 11-3 win over teammate Zhou Qihao and next faces India's Manav Thakkar, who survived a five-game battle against Flavien Coton. The biggest American result came when Kanak Jha upset No. 14 seed Shunsuke Togami 11-6, 12-10, 4-11, 7-11, 12-10 to reach the last 32, where he meets England's Tom Jarvis. In the women's draw, Sun Yingsha and Wang Manyu advanced in straight games, but No. 6 Chen Yi lost a five-setter to Ying Han. Lin Yun-ju also fell in the opening round as the No. 7 seed, losing to teammate Kuo Guan-hong. The tournament runs through July 5 across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles.
